questions.text: Who is the multi-percussionist discussed in Part I of the chapter?
questions.explanation: Part I tells the story of Evelyn Glennie, a deaf girl who became a world-renowned percussionist.

questions.text: At what age did Evelyn's deafness first become noticeable?
questions.explanation: Her mother noticed something was wrong when eight-year-old Evelyn didn't respond when her name was called to play piano.

questions.text: By what age was Evelyn's hearing completely impaired?
questions.explanation: Her hearing loss was confirmed by a specialist when she was eleven years old.

questions.text: Who was the percussionist that spotted Evelyn's potential and encouraged her?
questions.explanation: Ron Forbes noticed her talent and taught her to feel music through different parts of her body.

questions.text: How did Ron Forbes ask Evelyn to hear the music?
options: By feeling it some other way
questions.explanation: He told her, 'Don’t listen through your ears... try to sense it some other way.'

questions.text: Which prestigious music school did Evelyn audition for?
questions.explanation: She auditioned for the Royal Academy of Music in London and scored one of the highest marks in its history.

questions.text: In 1991, Evelyn was presented with which prestigious award?
questions.explanation: The Royal Philharmonic Society’s prestigious Soloist of the Year Award was given to her in 1991.

questions.text: What does Evelyn do before playing on a wooden platform?
questions.explanation: She removes her shoes so the vibrations pass through her bare feet and up her legs.

questions.text: Evelyn Glennie is a citizen of which country?
questions.explanation: The story mentions her 'fresh from a Scottish farm' background.

questions.text: Apart from regular concerts, where else does Evelyn give free concerts?
questions.explanation: She gives free concerts in prisons and hospitals to share the joy of music.

questions.text: Who is the master percussionist who said 'God may have taken her hearing but He has given her back something extraordinary'?
questions.explanation: James Blades made this famous remark about Evelyn's talent.

questions.text: Which instrument did Emperor Aurangzeb ban in his palace?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb banned the pungi because it had a shrill, unpleasant sound.

questions.text: Who improved the pungi and named it the 'Shehnai'?
options: A barber of a family of professional musicians
questions.explanation: A barber (nai) improved the instrument and played it in the Shah's chambers, hence 'Sheh-nai'.

questions.text: Where was Bismillah Khan born?
questions.explanation: Ustad Bismillah Khan was born in Dumraon, Bihar, in 1916.

questions.text: As a five-year-old, what did Bismillah Khan do in Dumraon?
questions.explanation: He used to play gilli-danda and sing Bhojpuri 'Chaita' in the local Bihariji temple.

questions.text: What was the reward Bismillah Khan earned for singing the Chaita?
questions.explanation: The local Maharaja would reward him with a 1.25 kg laddu.

questions.text: Bismillah Khan's maternal uncle, Ali Bux, was the official player of which temple?
questions.explanation: His uncle was the shehnai player at the Vishnu temple of Varanasi.

questions.text: At what age did Bismillah Khan accompany his uncle to the Allahabad Music Conference?
questions.explanation: He was fourteen when he performed at the Allahabad conference, gaining praise from Ustad Faiyaz Khan.

questions.text: Which opening in 1938 became a big break for Bismillah Khan?
questions.explanation: The opening of All India Radio in Lucknow in 1938 provided him frequent opportunities to play.

questions.text: Bismillah Khan was the first Indian to greet the nation with his Shehnai on:
questions.explanation: He played at the Red Fort on the day India gained independence.

questions.text: Which raga did Bismillah Khan play on 15 August 1947?
questions.explanation: He poured his heart out into Raag Kafi from the Red Fort to an audience including Nehru.

questions.text: Which film director was so impressed by Bismillah Khan that he named a film after the instrument?
questions.explanation: Vijay Bhatt named his film 'Gunj Uthi Shehnai' after hearing Bismillah play at a festival.

questions.text: Why did Bismillah Khan refuse to start a Shehnai school in the USA?
options: He couldn't leave Benaras and the Ganga
questions.explanation: His deep love for the holy city of Benaras and the river Ganga made him refuse to live abroad.

questions.text: In 2001, Bismillah Khan was awarded India’s highest civilian award:
questions.explanation: Ustad Bismillah Khan was awarded the Bharat Ratna in 2001.

questions.text: Evelyn Glennie says, 'If you work hard and know where you are going, you'll _______.'
questions.explanation: This is her mantra for success and perseverance.

questions.text: What does Evelyn feel when she plays the xylophone?
options: Sound passing up the stick into her fingertips
questions.explanation: She senses the sound passing up the sticks into her fingertips.

questions.text: Evelyn can speak flawlessly with which accent?
questions.explanation: She has a Scottish lilt because she could hear until the age of eleven.

questions.text: How many instruments can Evelyn play?
questions.explanation: She has mastered some thousand instruments as a multi-percussionist.

questions.text: Bismillah Khan’s father was a shehnai player in whose court?
questions.explanation: His father, Paigambar Bux, was a great shehnai player in the Dumraon court.

questions.text: The Shehnai was traditionally part of the 'naubat'. What is 'naubat'?
questions.explanation: Naubat is the traditional ensemble of nine instruments found at royal courts.

questions.text: Who brought the Shehnai onto the classical stage?
questions.explanation: Bismillah Khan is credited with bringing the instrument from temples to the classical concert stage.

questions.text: Bismillah Khan’s first trip abroad was to which country?
questions.explanation: His first foreign trip was to Afghanistan, where King Zahir Shah gifted him precious carpets.

questions.text: The Lincoln Center Hall, where Bismillah Khan performed, is in which country?
questions.explanation: The Lincoln Center Hall is located in the United States of America.

questions.text: An auditorium in Tehran was named after Bismillah Khan. What was its name?
questions.explanation: It was named 'Tahar Mosiquee Ustad Bismillah Khan' in his honor.

questions.text: Which of these awards did Bismillah Khan NOT receive?
questions.explanation: He received all major Indian civilian awards but not an Oscar.

questions.text: What does Bismillah Khan say about Indian music?
options: It is the world's richest heritage
questions.explanation: He believed Indian music is the richest heritage and even westerners come to learn it.

questions.text: What were the 'banks' Bismillah Khan loved to practice on?
questions.explanation: The flowing waters of the Ganga inspired him to improvise and invent ragas.

questions.text: Evelyn Glennie’s story is a source of inspiration for:
questions.explanation: She has given enormous pleasure to millions and is a beacon of hope for the disabled.

questions.text: The word 'captivate' in the text means:
questions.explanation: In context, Bismillah Khan's music captivated (fascinated) the King of Afghanistan.

questions.text: Part II of the chapter emphasizes that Bismillah Khan was a symbol of:
questions.explanation: He was a devout Muslim who played every morning at the Kashi Vishwanath temple, symbolizing India's composite culture.
